contextBanner: [TBG | Harness Skill Maturity | coordinator/architecture | branch: docs/agent-skills-stale-pr-cherry-pick]
purpose: Decide whether a proposed change should thicken the harness, remain a narrow skill/domain behavior, or be rejected as ceremony.
allowedScope: classifying cross-cutting plumbing such as config, dependency injection, capability routing, permission gates, evidence capture, retries, rollback, metrics, English/JSON reporting, UI shims, schemas, and adapters
moving repeated orchestration into workflow contracts or harness policy when current repo evidence shows duplication or drift
moving stateless transforms and domain decisions into narrow skills or domain modules
adding registries and manifests that let agents load narrow context instead of large prompt bodies
recording harness-maturity decisions in architecture docs without changing runtime behavior
forbiddenScope: moving domain behavior into harness solely to chase a percentage
rewriting runtime services while doing an architecture-only maturity sprint
claiming live runtime proof from static harness or skill changes
bypassing existing workflow contracts, operator catalogs, policy resolvers, or evidence rules
adding generic frameworks without a current duplication, safety, replay, or agent-load problem

when: the logic applies across workflows, agents, runners, or engines
the logic enforces safety, permissions, evidence, retries, rollback, reporting, schemas, or adapter behavior
the logic reduces duplicated orchestration or stale prompt/context drift
the logic is stateless, side-effect-free, or domain-specific
the logic performs a game/economy/route/smithing decision rather than enforcing a cross-cutting guard
the logic can be tested as a small pure function or bounded service
the only reason is to increase the harness percentage
the change adds ceremony without a safety, replay, audit, or agent-load benefit
the proposed refactor crosses into forbidden runtime scope

orderedSteps.name: identify_pain_point
requiredEvidence: duplicated orchestration, unsafe mutation path, stale prompt load, missing registry, missing evidence, missing rollback, or unclear ownership

orderedSteps.name: classify_logic
requiredEvidence: proof that executable contracts or current source remain authoritative

orderedSteps.name: choose_minimal_surface
requiredEvidence: manifest, contract, policy, skill doc, validator, or domain file chosen intentionally
reason broader framework work is not required

orderedSteps.name: validate_boundary
requiredEvidence: no runtime claims from static work
no domain behavior hidden inside harness
validation commands or skipped checks recorded

requires: git diff --check passes or exact local blocker is recorded
